title: Level raising mod 2 and arbitrary 2-Selmer ranks
speaker: Chao Li
abstract: We prove a level raising mod $p = 2$ theorem for elliptic curves over $\mathbb Q$, generalizing theorems of Ribet and Diamond-Taylor. As an application, we show that the 2-Selmer rank can be arbitrary in level raising families. We will begin by explaining our motivation from W. Zhang's approach to the $p$-part of the BSD conjecture. Explicit examples will be given to illustrate different phenomena compared to odd $p$. This is joint work with Bao V. Le Hung.

proof_stages.label: BSD motivation and rank-lowering target
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls refined BSD, explains why the 2-primary part is arithmetically important, and reformulates Wei Zhang's rank-one strategy as requiring mod-2 level raising, rank lowering, and ultimately a Jochnowitz congruence.

proof_stages.label: Classical level-raising framework
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines level-raising primes through the residual Galois representation, recalls Ribet's sufficiency theorem and Diamond-Taylor simultaneous level raising for odd primes, and explains how odd-prime congruences control local signs.

proof_stages.label: Corrected mod-2 simultaneous level raising with signs
proof_stages.summary: Because signs are invisible modulo 2 and the naive odd-prime expectations fail, the main mod-2 theorem asserts simultaneous level raising with prescribed signs at all relevant primes except possibly one prechosen prime dividing the original conductor.

proof_stages.label: Proof mechanism for mod-2 level raising
proof_stages.summary: The proof replaces the Diamond-Taylor argument at 2 by connected-object methods in the supersingular case and ordinary modularity lifting in the ordinary case, first allowing an auxiliary ramified prime and then removing it by quadratic twisting, where quadratic reciprocity leaves exactly one uncontrolled sign.

proof_stages.label: Selmer comparison from the mod-2 congruence
proof_stages.summary: For a level-raised modular abelian variety A, the congruence identifies E[2] with A[λ], so the 2-Selmer group of E and the λ-Selmer group of A become subspaces of one cohomology group defined by comparable local Kummer conditions.

proof_stages.label: Arbitrary Selmer ranks in the level-raising family
proof_stages.summary: Using infinitely many level-raising primes and the Selmer local-condition comparison, the speaker states that, under the standing hypotheses and negative discriminant, infinitely many level-raised abelian varieties realize any prescribed λ-Selmer rank.

proof_stages.label: Base-change obstruction to naive rank lowering
proof_stages.summary: When the auxiliary imaginary quadratic field needed for the Heegner-point/Jochnowitz strategy is imposed, a curve with Selmer rank one over that field cannot be level-raised once to Selmer rank zero; the rank instead becomes two, revealing a 2-primary Sha-jumping obstruction.

examples.note: The conductor-11 elliptic curve 11a and its level-77 congruent form illustrate Ribet level raising, the congruence away from the raised prime, and the odd-prime sign rules already stated.

examples.name: 11a level-raised curves over Heegner quadratic fields
examples.note: The computed table for 11a over small imaginary quadratic fields illustrates the obstruction theorem, including cases where Mordell-Weil rank is zero but the 2-part of Tate-Shafarevich jumps to keep Selmer rank two.
examples.engagement_reason: he computes tables for 11a level-raised at various primes over Heegner quadratic fields, comparing ranks, Sha, and 2-Selmer ranks and interpreting the observed jumps
